prompt: I'm adding usage-based billing to the DevDigest backend and haven't written any code yet. It needs to: (1) pull subscription + past invoices from Stripe, (2) compute proration on mid-cycle upgrade, (3) validate the upgrade request body, (4) persist invoices to Postgres, (5) expose GET /billing/summary and POST /billing/upgrade. How should I structure this across the module — where does each piece live and how do they talk?

assertions.text: Places Stripe access in an adapter (src/adapters/) wired through the Container, consumed via container — not instantiated in the service.
Places the proration computation as a pure domain function (no I/O), orchestrated by the service.
Puts request-body validation as a Zod HTTP schema in routes.ts (presentation layer).
Persists invoices via a repository (infra), workspace-scoped, mapping rows to a domain DTO.
Describes endpoints as thin handlers: getContext -> one service call -> reply.
Puts shared Invoice/Plan types in vendor/shared/contracts with zero framework imports.
States cross-module data access must go through the other module's service, not its repository.
